channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Iran war boosts Beijing ahead of Trump visit, experts say
item.description: As US President Donald Trump prepares to meet his Chinese counterpart in Beijing, some analysts say the war in Iran has significantly shifted the power dynamics in China's favour.
item.dc:creator: Brad Ryan and Emilie Gramenz

media:group.media:description: As Donald Trump left for Beijing, he said he would talk to China's president about the war but did not need help to end it.

item.title: Former national rugby captain banned over urine-swapping drug test scheme
item.description: Former Georgia captain Merab Sharikadze was one of six players handed lengthy bans from rugby for their part in a scheme involving alleged sample substitution and advance warnings of drug tests.

media:group.media:description: Merab Sharikadze is one of six players who have been banned for anti-doping violations.

item.title: Man injured while allegedly blowing up ATM at Queen Victoria Market
item.description: Police say a man is under police guard in a Melbourne hospital after he allegedly blew up an ATM on Wednesday morning.

media:group.media:description: Police said three people used explosives to access an ATM near Queen Victoria Market.

item.title: Voss says Carlton in a better place than when he took charge
item.description: Michael Voss has struck a defiant tone after leaving Carlton, insistent the AFL club is in better shape than when he started as coach.

media:group.media:description: Former Carlton coach Michael Voss has spoken out after resigning from the Blues.

item.title: Police attend 160 family violence call-outs in Melbourne's south in one week
item.description: Police say a man who threatened to kill his wife with a hammer and tried to set fire to a property was just one of dozens of arrests for family violence in a single week in the Dandenong area.

media:group.media:description: Police in Dandenong responded to 160 call outs in a single week related to family violence last month.

item.title: Breaking: 'Toxic': Plan to build 91-storey Trump Tower on Gold Coast scrapped
item.description: The branded tower proposed for Surfers Paradise would have been Australia's tallest building.
item.dc:creator: Emily Dobson

media:group.media:description: The plan to build a Trump Tower on the Gold Coast has been abandoned.

item.title: Live: ASX set to edge lower in first session after budget, Wall Street mixed
item.description: As the nation digests the budget, Australian shares are set for another day of losses, with the SPI futures pointing to 0.1 per cent fall by the ASX 200 after a mixed night on Wall Street. Follow our live markets blog.
item.dc:creator: Jason Dasey

media:group.media:description: ASX Board. Bridge st Sydney

item.title: Live: Chalmers says $250 tax perk for working Australians could help future bracket creep
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers says the offset will allow future governments to return bracket creep to working Australians in the form of an offset. Follow live.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Boscaini and Caitlin Rawling

media:group.media:description: Anthony Albanese and Jim Chalmers walk out to speak to media on day after budget.

item.title: 'Something catastrophic is going to happen': NT mayor on car thefts spike
item.description: Katherine's mayor says residents feel less safe than they did a year ago and is calling on Northern Territory Chief Minister Lia Finocchiaro to hold an "urgent" roundtable on crime in the Top End town.
item.dc:creator: James Elton

media:group.media:description: Joanna Holden is calling on the chief minister to visit Katherine to lead a roundtable on crime.

item.title: Sinner equals Djokovic's 31-match Masters record at Italian Open
item.description: World number one Jannik Sinner extended his Masters 1000 winning streak to 31 matches, equalling Novak Djokovic's all-time record.

media:group.media:description: Jannik Sinner is through to the quarterfinals of the Italian Open.

item.title: Farmers, environmentalists label federal budget 'savage' and 'tragic'
item.description: Weed and pest control programs have been cut in the federal budget as farmers and conservationists grapple with feral animals and noxious plants.
item.dc:creator: Fiona Broom

media:group.media:description: Rabbits cause costly damage on farms.

item.title: Midnight Oil's Peter Garrett 'closes the circle' to chair Landcare
item.description: The former frontman for the Australian rockers was among those who helped lobby for the formation of Landcare Australia in 1989, so it seems fitting he should circle back to chair the organisation after a lifetime lobbying for the environment.

media:group.media:description: Peter Garrett is the new Landcare Australia chairperson.

item.title: Live: Trump says US has Iran 'very much under control' ahead of Beijing visit
item.description: US President Donald Trump says he does not need help from Beijing to end the war with Iran but says he will talk at length with Chinese President Xi Jinping when they hold a summit this week. Follow live.

media:group.media:description: Donald Trump talks with reporters as he departs the White House for travel to Beijing.

item.title: High Court to hear appeal over 22-year Mount Pleasant coal mine extension
item.description: The High Court will hear a major climate change case that could affect mining approvals, as the operator of the Mount Pleasant coal mine seeks to overturn a ruling blocking the project's extension.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Byrne

media:group.media:description: The expansion of MACH Energy's Mount Pleasant coal mine would result in it continuing to operate until 2048

item.title: 'We are heartbroken': NBA in shock after death of Grizzlies forward
item.description: Memphis Grizzlies forward Clarke has died, the team, his agency and the NBA have confirmed.

media:group.media:description: Memphis Grizzlies forward Brandon Clarke was arrested recently for speeding and possession of a controlled substance.

item.title: Three-storey seniors unit block a threat to 'peaceful' street, locals say
item.description: New public housing units built specifically for seniors are under construction in Sydney's south-west, despite opposition from some residents.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Ross

media:group.media:description: Almost a third of households on the social housing waitlist are seniors, with a new unit block almost ready in Riverwood.

item.title: LIV Golf has triggered a turf war in Adelaide's park lands — here are the battlelines
item.description: The felling of hundreds of trees on the edge of the Adelaide CBD has seen a war erupt between the state government and environmentalists. Here's a look at how things have got here.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Keane

media:group.media:description: A protester with a possum puppet at the North Adelaide Golf Course.

item.title: Australian-born MP at centre of 'palace coup' plot to knife British PM
item.description: Up until the past few days, Catherine West kept a low profile, as far as politicians go. Most Brits would never have come across the Australian-born, London-based MP before, but that all changed at the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Riley Stuart

media:group.media:description: Catherine West was born country Victoria, but has served as an MP in north London for more than a decade.

item.title: Emergency department nurse faces deportation due to son's condition
item.description: A Victorian emergency department nurse and her family face deportation before the end of the month due to her six-year-old son's developmental delay.
item.dc:creator: Anna McGuinness

media:group.media:description: Christin Das, Hazel, Jineesh Joseph and Jaziel are asking the minister to intervene in their case.

item.title: This duck was 'effectively wiped out' from NZ. There are fears Tasmania's next
item.description: The crossbreeding of Pacific black ducks with introduced species has devastated the native species in New Zealand — and conservationists say there's a risk that's happening in Tasmania too.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie Heard

media:group.media:description: For those wanting to keep ducks on their properties, it is recommended Muscovy ducks and guinea fowls, which don't pose the same hybridisation risk as mallards.

item.title: How fuel prices have impacted car sales
item.description: Electric vehicles now represent 16 per cent of new car sales in Australia, in a growing trend coinciding with record fuel prices.
item.dc:creator: Liana Walker

media:group.media:description: Rising fuel costs have had little impact on sales of big cars.

item.title: Another Aussie out of Giro as UAE Team Emirates hits back
item.description: Jhonatan Narváez dedicated his Giro d'Italia stage four victory to his fallen UAE Team Emirates colleagues as another Aussie fell by the wayside as the race hit Italian soil.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale

media:group.media:description: Kaden Groves was banged up and far from his best after the first-stage crash.

item.title: Australia insists support for UAE does not involve action against Iran
item.description: The Albanese government insists Australia is not involved in any "offensive action against Iran" amid reports the UAE, which had been supplied a Wedgetail aircraft, carried out military strikes against the country.
item.dc:creator: Laura Tingle

media:group.media:description: Australia's E-7A Wedgetail aircraft have a radar with a range of 400 kilometres.

item.title: How Australians across generations feel about the budget
item.description: The ABC has asked Australians across generations, from gen Z to baby boomers, about the latest federal budget.
item.dc:creator: Ahmed Yussuf

media:group.media:description: The ABC spoke to people across generations to find out how they feel about the federal budget.

item.title: Will property tax changes make housing cheaper for first home buyers?
item.description: There's no doubt that taking away negative gearing on existing properties will reduce the amount many investors can afford to pay, evening up the playing field for owner-occupiers. But these tax changes by themselves aren't a cure all.
item.dc:creator: Michael Janda

media:group.media:description: Anthony Albanese and Jim Chalmers have decided to take a "seek forgiveness, not permission" approach to housing changes.
